data.title: Ringgit Opens Lower Against Dollar, Mixed Against Others
data.description:
The ringgit opened lower against the US dollar and was mixed against other currencies, as support from firmer oil prices was offset by broad dollar strength and cautious positioning following the US Federal Reserve (Fed)’s rate decision, Bernama reported.

At 8.04 am, the domestic unit eased against the greenback to 3.9565/9630, from 3.9495/9540 at Wednesday’s close.

Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said the Fed was inclined to keep rates steady, as economic activity continued to expand at a solid pace despite slower job growth.

At its third meeting of the year, the Federal Open Market Committee maintained the target range for the federal funds rate at 3.50 per cent to 3.75 per cent.

“With the sharp spike in crude oil prices, the ringgit is likely to weaken today, with the US dollar/ringgit pair potentially attempting to breach RM3.96,” Mohd Afzanizam told Bernama.

At the opening, the ringgit was traded mixed against a basket of major currencies, and against its ASEAN peers.

It rose slightly against the Japanese yen to 2.4700/4744 from 2.4709/4739 on Wednesday, but weakened against the British pound to 5.3381/3469 from 5.3330/3391 and slipped against the euro to 4.6236/6312 from 4.6197/6250.

It edged down against the Singapore dollar to 3.0905/0659 from 3.0909/0946 at Wednesday’s close, but strengthened against the Thai baht to 12.0765/1022 from 12.0791/0980.

The ringgit eased against the Indonesian rupiah to 228.3/228.8 from 227.9/228.3 and fell against the Philippine peso to 6.42/6.44 from 6.41/6.42.

data.title: Stock Picks: Telekom And MRCB
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) has identified Telekom Malaysia Bhd and Malaysian Resources Corp Bhd (MRCB) as stocks to watch, citing strong bullish momentum and potential for further upside following recent technical breakouts.

The research house said Telekom Malaysia may continue its upward trajectory after breaking above the RM7.60 resistance level on strong volume, supported by two consecutive bullish candlesticks.

RHB Research noted that the breakout signals strengthening momentum, with the stock likely to extend gains towards RM8, followed by RM8.25 if buying interest persists. However, it cautioned that a drop below the RM7.35 support level would invalidate the bullish setup.

Meanwhile, MRCB is also showing continued upside momentum after staging a breakout above the RM0.33 resistance level.

The stock printed a bullish candlestick on high volume, indicating sustained buying interest, with RHB Research expecting follow-through gains towards RM0.36 and subsequently RM0.39.

That said, the research house warned that a fall below the RM0.30 support level could trigger a correction phase and negate the current bullish outlook.

data.title: Singapore Shares Start Strong, STI Up Nearly 1% In Early Trade
data.description:
Singapore shares opened higher, with the Straits Times Index (STI) rising 0.96% to 4,907.82 in early trade as investors tracked a mixed Wall Street close and ongoing global market uncertainty.

Market breadth was slightly positive at the open, with 108 gainers against 106 decliners. Total early turnover stood at 153.63 million shares worth S$302.62 million.

Sentiment was supported by a steadier tone in global equities after US markets ended mixed, as investors weighed the US Federal Reserve’s decision to hold interest rates steady and concerns over rising oil prices amid geopolitical tensions in the Middle East.

Energy-linked volatility remained in focus after crude prices climbed on supply disruption fears linked to potential restrictions in the Strait of Hormuz.

On Wall Street, the Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 0.57%, while the S&P 500 edged down 0.04% and the Nasdaq Composite inched up 0.04%, reflecting a cautious risk environment ahead of major technology earnings.

Regional derivatives trade was active, with MSCI Singapore Index Futures for May 2026 last at 438.00.

data.title: Asian Stocks Rebound As Earnings Optimism Builds Ahead Of Fed Decision
data.description:
Asian markets edged higher as investor sentiment improved on stronger-than-expected corporate earnings, even as attention shifted to the upcoming US Federal Reserve decision and ongoing geopolitical tensions.

MSCI’s broadest index of Asia-Pacific shares outside Japan rose 0.2%, reversing earlier losses as gains in Hong Kong steadied the benchmark, while Japanese markets were closed for a holiday. US S&P 500 e-mini futures also gained 0.2%.

Market optimism was underpinned by resilient US corporate earnings, with about 81% of S&P 500 companies that have reported so far beating estimates. Investors are now closely watching results from major technology firms including Microsoft, Alphabet, Amazon and Meta Platforms, which are expected to test the sustainability of the AI-driven rally.

“For us, earnings are the most important part of the story right now,” said Kate Moore, chief investment officer at Citi Wealth. “Q1 earnings are tracking year-over-year growth and climbing. Analysts typically spend earnings season revising numbers down. This season, the opposite appears to be happening.”

However, technology stocks came under pressure earlier after reports that OpenAI missed internal targets for users and revenue, raising concerns over the pace of growth in the AI sector. This weighed on broader US indices, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq declining in the previous session.

Geopolitical developments also remained in focus, with US President Donald Trump reportedly pushing for an extended blockade of Iran, prolonging tensions in the Middle East and supporting oil prices. Brent crude rose 0.2% to US$111.51 per barrel.

Investors are now awaiting the outcome of the Federal Reserve’s meeting, with markets widely expecting rates to be held steady amid inflation concerns linked to the conflict.

Reuters

data.title: SGX Opens Lower, STI Slips 0.39% Amid Global Tech And Oil Jitters
data.description:
Singapore shares opened weaker, tracking a broad pullback on Wall Street as renewed concerns over artificial intelligence-driven valuations and volatile oil prices weighed on sentiment.

At 9.15am, the Straits Times Index (STI) fell 19.06 points or 0.39% to 4,868.63, with early trade reflecting cautious risk appetite across regional markets. Total market turnover stood at 197.58 million securities worth S$222.37 million, while 118 stocks advanced against 112 decliners.

Regional sentiment was influenced by a weaker overnight session in the United States, where the Nasdaq Composite dropped 0.90% to 24,663.80, marking its sharpest daily fall in a month. The S&P 500 slipped 0.49% to 7,138.80, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average edged down 0.05% to 49,141.93.

Technology shares led losses in the US after renewed concerns over the sustainability of the artificial intelligence boom ahead of major earnings from big tech firms. Semiconductor stocks, which have rallied more than 40% this year, were among the hardest hit.

Sentiment was further pressured after a Wall Street Journal report raised questions about OpenAI’s growth trajectory and its heavy data centre spending requirements. Major chip-related names including Nvidia, AMD and Broadcom declined between 1.6% and 4.4%, while Oracle fell 4.1% and CoreWeave dropped 5.8%.

“(OpenAI) is giving investors more food for thought, whether the growth is slowing and what that means for capex spending,” said Chuck Carlson, chief executive officer at Horizon Investment Services.

Attention now turns to a packed earnings week for US megacap technology firms, with Alphabet, Amazon, Meta Platforms and Microsoft scheduled to report, followed by Apple. Together, these companies account for roughly 44% of the S&P 500’s market capitalisation.

Back in Singapore, broader regional sentiment was also shaped by rising oil price volatility and geopolitical tensions, which have revived inflation concerns and kept investors cautious on risk assets.

Market participants are also watching upcoming signals from the US Federal Reserve, which is widely expected to keep interest rates unchanged while assessing the inflation impact of elevated energy prices and global uncertainty.

data.title: Hang Seng Futures Rally Faces Key Resistance At 26,600
data.description:
Hang Seng Index Futures (HSIF) extended its counter-trend rebound, closing 233 points higher at 25,940, according to RHB Investment Bank (RHB Research).

The index opened at 25,549 and dipped to a low of 25,439 before gaining momentum to hit an intraday high of 26,009, supported by renewed bullish sentiment.

RHB said the HSIF has now broken above both its 20-day and 50-day simple moving averages, with the shorter-term average crossing above the longer-term line, signalling near-term strength.

Despite the rebound, the research house maintained a bearish trading stance, noting that resistance at 26,600 remains a key ceiling. The index is expected to face selling pressure and potentially retrace towards the 25,100 support level.

Traders are advised to maintain short positions initiated at 26,367, with a stop-loss set at 26,600 to manage downside risks.

Immediate support is seen at 25,100, followed by 24,500, while resistance levels are pegged at 26,600 and 27,200.

data.title: Decliners Dominate Bursa As 617 Losers Drag Market Lower At Close
data.description:
Decliners led the charge on FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) at the close on April 29, with 617 stocks ending in the red against 485 gainers, reflecting broad-based weakness across the market.

Trading sentiment softened as turnover fell to 3.63 billion units worth RM3.13 billion, down from 3.85 billion units valued at RM3.38 billion in the previous session, signalling reduced investor appetite.

Among the top losers, Hong Leong Industries Bhd dropped 30 sen to RM17.50, while Petronas Dagangan Bhd declined 26 sen to RM20.30. Malayan Banking Bhd also weighed on sentiment, sliding 18 sen to RM11.04.

Other notable decliners included SAM Engineering & Equipment (M) Bhd, down 21 sen to RM3.97, and Petronas Gas Bhd, which shed 14 sen to RM17.82.

On the upside, gains were led by Nestlé (M) Berhad, which rose 60 sen to RM102.90, followed by Malaysian Pacific Industries Bhd, up 50 sen to RM38.

Despite selective buying in heavyweight counters, negative breadth and weaker turnover underscored a cautious mood as investors trimmed positions across sectors.

data.title: Ringgit Opens Stronger Against US Dollar Amid Volatile Oil Prices Near US$100
data.description:
The ringgit strengthened against the US dollar but was mixed against other currencies in early trade today, amid cautious sentiment over oil price volatility and developments in the Strait of Hormuz, an analyst said.

At 8.03 am, the local currency stood at 3.9475/9540 against the greenback, compared with 3.9505/9550 at Tuesday’s close.

Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said the ringgit is expected to trade within a narrow range as market participants await further clarity on the situation in the Strait of Hormuz.

At the time of writing, oil prices remained volatile following the United Arab Emirates’ exit from the OPEC alliance. West Texas Intermediate fell 0.83 per cent to US$99.10 per barrel, while Brent crude slipped 0.59 per cent to US$110.60 per barrel.

“News of the UAE’s departure from OPEC in May, after more than six decades of membership, is set to reshape the oil and gas landscape. The country will be free to determine its output levels in line with its investment in the sector,” Mohd Afzanizam told Bernama.

However, for now, the focus is on supply cuts amid the war in Iran, which has disrupted the flow of oil and gas through the Strait of Hormuz, he added.

Mohd Afzanizam said US data showed the Conference Board’s Consumer Confidence Index rose to 92.8 in April following a ceasefire in the Iran conflict.

However, he added that expectations of a US recession over the next 12 months have risen, while plans to purchase big-ticket items have declined.

“Tonight, the Federal Open Market Committee’s decision will be closely watched for signals on whether policymakers are becoming more wary of growth prospects amid a higher inflation environment,” he said.

At the opening, the ringgit was mixed against a basket of major currencies.

It rose against the Japanese yen to 2.4740/4782 from Tuesday’s 2.4756/4785, but weakened against the British pound to 5.3394/3482 from 5.3292/3353, and slipped against the euro to 4.6261/6337 from 4.6189/6242.

The local currency traded mostly higher against ASEAN peers.

It edged up against the Singapore dollar to 3.0937/0990 from 3.0943/0983 at Tuesday’s close and strengthened against the Thai baht to 12.1484/1759 from 12.1494/1685.

The ringgit was little changed against the Indonesian rupiah at 228.9/229.4 and was steady against the Philippine peso at 6.44/6.45 from 6.44/6.46.

Bernama

data.title: Bursa Closes Lower As Oil Stocks Retreat On UAE’s OPEC+ Exit Move
data.description:
The Bursa Malaysia ended lower on April 29 as investors locked in gains on oil-related counters after news that the United Arab Emirates (UAE) plans to withdraw from OPEC+, triggering concerns over crude market stability.

The benchmark index fell 9.18 points, or 0.53%, to close at 1,720.42, retreating from its 1,729.60 finish on April 28 after trading between 1,718.45 and 1,733.84.

Broader market sentiment was also weaker, with the FBM Emas shedding 55.54 points (0.43%) to 12,743.20, while the FBM 70 declined 71.87 points (0.39%) to 18,198.10.

Among index heavyweights, Malayan Banking Bhd slid 18 sen to RM11.04, CIMB Group Holdings Bhd lost nine sen to RM7.69 and Public Bank Bhd eased three sen to RM4.75. Offsetting some losses, Tenaga Nasional Bhd rose eight sen to RM14.60, while IHH Healthcare Bhd ended unchanged at RM8.81.

Trading activity remained brisk, led by Borneo Oil Bhd, UEM Sunrise Bhd and Malaysian Resources Corporation Bhd among the day’s most active stocks.

data.title: Bursa Malaysia Ends Higher, FBMKLCI Up
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia ended higher today, with key indices closing in positive territory amid improved market sentiment.

The benchmark FBMKLCI rose 12.33 points, or 0.72%, to 1,729.60.

Broader market indices also advanced, with the FBM70 gaining 100.90 points (+0.56%) to 18,269.97, while the FBMEMAS climbed 82.61 points (+0.65%) to 12,798.74.

The FBMSHA added 69.10 points (+0.55%) to 12,729.19, reflecting gains across small-cap stocks.

Meanwhile, the F4GBM index increased 6.49 points, or 0.63%, to 1,033.86.

Among the active stocks being traded are Luster Industries Bhd slipped 0.5 sen to 3.5 sen with 117.1 million shares traded and Zetrix AI Bhd fell 1 sen to 86 sen with 104.1 million shares traded.

Other active stocks are UEM Sunrise Bhd, Malaysian Resources Corporation Bhd and VS Industry Bhd.

The mixed performance reflects selective buying interest as investors rotated between sectors during the session.

data.title: Central Banks ‘Scoop Up A Load’ Of Gold In Bumpy First Quarter
data.description:
Central banks added gold holdings at the fastest pace in more than a year in the first quarter, as a slump in prices encouraged a wave of buying that more than offset sales by a handful of institutions.

Net official-sector purchases totalled 244 tons in the three months, up from 208 tons in the previous quarter, according to estimates from the World Gold Council, an industry body. Poland, Uzbekistan and China were the largest reported buyers, although some other purchases were undeclared.

Gold prices have swung widely this year, hitting a record in late January before slumping in March after the outbreak of the US-Iran war. Among factors weighing on bullion were soaring energy prices, which raised expectations that central banks will keep borrowing costs steady or even hike them to contain inflation. That is a headwind for the metal, which does not bear interest.

“It’s the first time in a while that we’ve seen a decent correction in gold,” said John Reade, chief strategist at the London-based World Gold Council. “That has allowed central banks that might have been hanging back, waiting for exactly this opportunity, to come in and scoop up a load.”

The jump in net accumulation is especially marked given several central banks pared holdings. Turkey, Russia and Azerbaijan joined a number of other smaller banks and sovereign wealth funds in shedding an estimated 115 tons over the period.

At the time, the moves raised concerns about institutions’ continued appetite for gold, a trend that has been a key driver of its multi-year rally. Each bank had its own motivation for selling: Turkey to shield its currency and economy from the effects of the war, Russia to meet a budget deficit, and Azerbaijan to bring holdings back within permitted limits.

Spot bullion was trading a little below US$4,600 an ounce shortly ahead of the World Gold Council’s report. Prices peaked at a record near US$5,600 on Jan 29, then went on to shed 12% in March to cap the biggest monthly drop since 2008.

Much of the central-bank buying included in the World Gold Council’s figures is not disclosed and not included in International Monetary Fund statistics. Consultancy Metals Focus Ltd. calculates estimated purchases on behalf of the World Gold Council using a combination of public data, trade statistics and field research.

Bloomberg

data.title: Bursa Slips At Midday As Oil Jitters Drag Energy And Bank Stocks
data.description:
FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) ended the morning session in the red, mirroring weaker regional markets as investor sentiment soured on reports that the United Arab Emirates may exit OPEC+, raising fresh uncertainty over global oil supply dynamics.

At 12:30pm, the FBM KLCI fell 9.20 points, or 0.53%, to 1,720.40, retreating from its close of 1,729.60 on April 28 as losses in oil-linked and financial services counters weighed on the benchmark.

The broader market also softened, with the FBM Emas down 51.15 points (0.4%) to 12,747.59, the FBM 70 easing 24.45 points (0.13%) to 18,245.52, the FBM Shariah slipping 27.89 points (0.22%) to 12,701.30 and the F4GBM declining 4.61 points (0.45%) to 1,029.25.

Market breadth remained negative, with 514 decliners outpacing 422 gainers, while 589 counters were unchanged.

Among the biggest drags, Petronas Dagangan Bhd shed 40 sen to RM20.16, while Hong Leong Bank Bhd slipped 16 sen to RM22.36 and Hong Leong Financial Group Bhd declined 12 sen to RM18.92.

On the upside, Malaysian Pacific Industries Bhd led gainers, climbing RM1.38 to RM38.88, followed by IOI Properties Group Bhd and ITMAX System Bhd.

Active trading centred on construction and technology-linked counters, with UEM Sunrise Bhd topping the volume chart, followed by Zetrix AI Bhd and WCT Holdings Bhd.

The weaker midday performance underscores lingering market sensitivity to global energy developments, as investors watch for further signals on oil prices and regional risk appetite.

data.title: Foreign Exchange Rates April 29, 2026
data.description:
The closing foreign exchange rates, sourced from Bank Negara Malaysia and recorded at 5pm on April 29, 2026, provide a comprehensive overview of currency fluctuations for the day.

These rates, crucial for investors and businesses alike, reflect the relative strength or weakness of major global currencies against the Malaysian Ringgit. They serve as a vital indicator for assessing international trade competitiveness, investment opportunities and overall economic trends in the global market.

The exchange rates are as shown below:

data.title: Bitcoin Dips, Ethereum Holds Firm In Latest Trade
data.description:
Bitcoin and Ethereum traded mixed on April 29 as investors navigated a risk-off mood tied to slowing momentum in global equities and anticipation around macroeconomic signals.

Bitcoin hovered around US$76,300-US$76,800, down roughly 1% to 3% over the past 24 hours, while Ethereum held firmer near US$2,290-US$2,300, posting marginal gains.

The pullback in Bitcoin reflects its growing correlation with tech stocks, with analysts noting the cryptocurrency is increasingly trading like a high-beta risk asset during major earnings cycles.

Despite the dip, Bitcoin remains up for April, suggesting buyers are still stepping in on weakness and reinforcing longer-term confidence.

Ethereum, meanwhile, showed relative resilience as market participants continued to view the token as fundamentally supported by institutional interest and ecosystem demand.

Its steadier performance around the US$2,300-mark contrasts with Bitcoin’s sharper swings, signalling selective strength in large-cap altcoins.

For now, the crypto market remains in consolidation mode, with traders closely watching macro developments and broader investor sentiment for the next directional trigger.

data.title: Wall Street Ends Mixed As Oil Surge, Fed Decision Keep Markets On Edge
data.description:
Wall Street ended mixed as investors weighed rising oil prices, the Federal Reserve’s interest rate decision and a wave of major technology earnings released after the close.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 0.57% to 48,861.81, while the S&P 500 slipped 0.04% to 7,135.98. The Nasdaq Composite edged up 0.04% to 24,673.24.

Markets fluctuated after the Federal Reserve held interest rates steady, with policymakers showing their most divided stance since 1992 amid uncertainty over inflation driven by higher energy prices.

Crude prices climbed after the White House confirmed that Donald Trump had instructed officials to prepare for a prolonged blockade of Iranian ports, raising concerns over continued supply disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz.

Investor focus also turned to earnings from major US technology firms, including Microsoft, Alphabet, Amazon and Meta Platforms, which reported results after the closing bell. In extended trading, Alphabet shares rose more than 3%, while Amazon and Microsoft fell over 3%, and Meta declined more than 6%.

Energy stocks led gains within the S&P 500, supported by higher oil prices, while utilities and materials sectors posted the steepest losses.

Among individual stocks, Robinhood Markets dropped 13.2% after missing profit expectations, while Starbucks jumped 8.5% after raising its annual forecast. Visa gained 8.3% on improved earnings guidance.

Data-storage firms rallied following a strong outlook from Seagate Technology, with its shares surging 11.1%.

On the economic front, US core capital goods orders rose 3.3% in March, marking the largest increase since June 2020, signalling resilience in corporate spending despite global uncertainties.

Trading volume on US exchanges totalled 16.37 billion shares, below the 20-day average of 17.81 billion.

Reuters

data.title: Eco-Shop Able To Hold Pricing Despite Cost Pressure
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) maintained a BUY call on Eco-Shop Marketing Bhd with a revised target price of RM1.80, pointing to earnings strength, while noting valuation support following recent share price weakness.

Eco-Shop Marketing posted solid 9MFY26 results, with RHB Research highlighting core net profit of RM192 million, up 23% year-on-year and in line with expectations at about three quarters of full-year forecasts.

Revenue rose modestly by 2% year-on-year to RM2.1 billion despite strong store expansion, while margin gains were supported by pricing adjustments and stronger house brand contribution.

The research house said gross profit margin expanded to 32.8% in 9MFY26, up 5.8 percentage points year-on-year, which more than offset higher operating costs including wage pressures. This led to a 23% rise in core net profit, with further improvement seen in the quarter as margins reached a record high driven by scale benefits and favourable foreign exchange movement.

RHB Research said the outlook remains supported by continued outlet expansion with around 100 new stores planned annually for FY26 and FY27, alongside resilient demand for value retail products amid consumer downtrading trends. It added that Eco-Shop is well positioned to maintain pricing discipline despite cost inflation pressures from freight and geopolitical-related input costs.

However, the research house cautioned that risks include weaker same-store sales growth following earlier price increases and potential delays in expansion plans. It said near-term margin stability is expected as operational scale and house brand penetration help offset external cost pressures, supporting earnings visibility going forward.

As of 11.27 am, the stock price gained 3.13% to RM1.32.

data.title: Bursa Ends Mixed As Consumer Staples Outperform While Tech Stocks Retreat
data.description:
Nestlé (Malaysia) Bhd spearheaded a consumer stocks rally on Bursa Malaysia today, lifting top gainers even as decliners held a slight edge in a choppy session marked by profit-taking in select industrials and financials.

At the close, gainers trailed losers amid balanced activity, with high-volume trades spotlighting rotational interest in consumer staples and select tech plays.

Nestlé soared RM3.18 or 3.18% to RM102.30 on 2,651,000 shares, underscoring resilient demand for everyday essentials.

Fraser & Neave Holdings Bhd climbed RM0.72 to RM31.40, while KESM Industries Bhd jumped RM0.46 to RM4.19 on elevated volume of 8,747,000 shares.

United Plantations Bhd added RM0.38 to RM32.00, and Dutch Lady Industries Bhd gained RM0.28 to RM32.78.

On the downside, selling pressure was concentrated in industrial and semiconductor-linked counters. Malayan Pacific Industries fell 38 sen to RM37.50 on heavy turnover of 12,758 lots, while UWC Berhad declined 15 sen to RM5.12 amid the session’s highest trading volume of 38,822 lots among the losers.

Other mid-cap decliners included Allianz Malaysia Bhd, down 36 sen to RM20.80, and Batu Kawan Bhd, which slipped 16 sen to RM20.98 on thin trading. Kelington Group Bhd also eased 16 sen to RM6.22, despite active turnover of 24,577 lots.

Overall, the session reflected a defensive tilt, with gains concentrated in consumer staples and selected plantation counters, while technology and industrial names faced continued profit-taking pressure.

data.title: Wall Street Dips From Highs With AI Uncertainty, Fed In Focus
data.description:
U.S. stocks closed lower, pulling back from record highs as renewed concerns over the artificial intelligence boom weighed on technology shares ahead of key earnings from major tech firms.

The Nasdaq Composite led losses, falling 0.90% to 24,663.80 in its biggest daily decline in a month, while the S&P 500 dropped 0.49% to 7,138.80 and the Dow Jones Industrial Average slipped 0.05% to 49,141.93. Semiconductor shares, which have surged more than 40% this year, dragged the Nasdaq lower.

Sentiment in the sector weakened after a Wall Street Journal report raised concerns over OpenAI’s weekly users and revenue, fuelling doubts about its ability to sustain heavy data centre spending. Shares of Oracle fell 4.1%, while chipmakers Nvidia, AMD and Broadcom declined between 1.6% and 4.4%. Nvidia-backed CoreWeave dropped 5.8%.

“(OpenAI) is giving investors more food for thought, whether the growth is slowing and what that means for capex spending,” said Chuck Carlson, chief executive officer at Horizon Investment Services. “You’ve got major hyperscalers coming out with results tomorrow, which probably gives investors even more reason to take a few chips off the table.”

Focus now turns to earnings from major technology firms, with Alphabet, Amazon, Meta Platforms and Microsoft set to report on Wednesday, followed by Apple on Thursday. These companies account for about 44% of the S&P 500’s total market capitalisation.

Elsewhere, corporate earnings painted a mixed picture. General Motors rose 1.3% after beating profit estimates and lifting its full-year forecast, while Coca-Cola gained 3.9% after raising its annual outlook. United Parcel Service fell 4.0% as higher fuel costs offset business improvements.

Broader sentiment was also shaped by rising oil prices amid geopolitical tensions, which have revived inflation concerns. Investors are closely watching the U.S. Federal Reserve, widely expected to keep interest rates unchanged, while assessing the impact of elevated energy prices on future policy.

“We know that the Fed is effectively on hold,” said Oliver Pursche, senior vice president at Wealthspire Advisors. “If oil prices remain elevated, does that create an environment where energy-related inflation is not being viewed as transitory any longer, but rather as something that has a very much longer-term impact and might therefore force the Fed to raise rates?”

data.title: Hang Seng Index Futures Lose Momentum, Sellers Take Control Below 25,700 Level
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) has maintained a negative trading bias on Hang Seng Index futures (HSIF), advising investors to keep short positions as bearish momentum strengthens.

The research house said HSIF extended its decline on Tuesday, falling 223 points to close at 25,707 points after slipping below the 50-day simple moving average support. The index opened at 25,937 points and briefly touched a high of 25,979 points before reversing sharply to a low of 25,622 points, forming a bearish candlestick. In the evening session, it dropped a further 76 points to last trade at 25,631 points.

RHB Research noted that the latest price action confirms persistent downward momentum, with the 26,600-point level reaffirmed as immediate resistance. It said as long as the index remains below the 50-day SMA line, the downward drift is expected to continue.

The firm added that a decisive break below the 25,100-point support could trigger stronger selling pressure and reinforce the broader bearish structure. The next support level is seen at 24,500 points.

RHB Research reiterated that traders should maintain the short position initiated at 26,367 points, with a stop-loss set at 26,600 points to manage risks.

data.title: Stock Picks: KSL And Lim Seong Hai Capital
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) has identified KSL Holdings and Lim Seong Hai Capital as potential trading ideas, citing improving technical momentum and bullish price action.

RHB Research said KSL Holdings is attempting to extend its counter-trend rebound after climbing above its 21-day simple moving average line. The stock recently bounced off the RM3 psychological support level on strong trading volume, signalling easing selling pressure.

The research house noted that a break above the RM3.15 resistance could see KSL resume its upward movement towards RM3.30, with further upside to RM3.45. However, it cautioned that a fall below RM3 would indicate a bearish breakout and could trigger renewed downside pressure.

Meanwhile, RHB Research said Lim Seong Hai Capital may continue its upward trajectory after breaking past the RM1.85 resistance on strong volume. The stock has printed two consecutive bullish candlesticks, suggesting strengthening momentum.

It added that the counter could extend gains towards RM2, followed by RM2.15 if the positive trend holds. On the downside, a drop below the RM1.70 support level would negate the bullish outlook.
